title: biscutella laevigata subsp. hispidissima
intro: Biscutella laevigata subsp. hispidissima, also known as the Rough Biscutella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and high blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.
